title: Editable Columns in Data Dictionary Table
story: As a data migration specialist, I want to edit and add data to columns in the Data Dictionary table, so that I can map fields from my old system and write notes as part of the data mapping exercise.
description:
The Data Dictionary and Migration table views need inline-editable columns so users can annotate Salesforce field metadata directly. Editable fields are defined per the Migration and Dictionary view documentation — each view exposes a different subset of columns for editing.

This enables users to enrich Salesforce object field data with custom mapping notes, legacy system references, and documentation without leaving the table view.
acceptance_criteria: Columns designated as editable in the Migration view documentation are inline-editable
Columns designated as editable in the Dictionary view documentation are inline-editable
User can add data to empty editable cells
ux_changes: Designated columns in the data table become inline-editable. Users can click into cells to add or modify values directly in the Migration and Dictionary views.
middleware_changes: Server-side handler saves user-edited column values back to the database when cells are modified.
data_model_changes: User-editable columns are stored alongside Salesforce metadata fields, requiring storage for custom annotations per object field per user.
implementation_notes: Editable fields are configured based on the Sprint 5 documentation for Migration and Dictionary table views. Implementation uses inline editing on the data table component.
comment_summary: Developer confirmed editable fields were implemented per the documentation spec.
